Name: spectral_latency_optimization
Title: Spectral Latency Optimization: Training-Free Test-Time Optimization via Norm-Preserving Subspace Projection
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that spectral latent steering can be used to optimize test-time latency in language models by projecting test-time updates onto a low-rank, norm-preserving subspace, ensuring strict computational budgets while preserving the optimization landscape's geometry.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works in training-free machine learning methods, such as Spectral Quantization Routing and Geometric Signal Separation. However, our approach differs in that it focuses on optimizing test-time latency using spectral latent steering, whereas existing methods focus on precision allocation and signal separation. Additionally, our approach is grounded in information-theoretic bounds and PAC-Bayes frameworks, providing a more rigorous and transparent approach to test-time optimization.
Abstract: We propose a training-free framework for optimizing test-time latency in language models using spectral latent steering. Our approach projects test-time updates onto a low-rank, norm-preserving subspace, ensuring strict computational budgets while preserving the optimization landscape's geometry. We theoretically ground our approach in information-theoretic bounds and PAC-Bayes frameworks, providing a rigorous and transparent method for test-time optimization. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, our approach matches or exceeds full-space perturbation baselines while reducing hidden-state perturbation overhead by over 70% and enforcing deterministic latency bounds.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed approach on single-GPU setups across mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks
Compare the performance of our approach with full-space perturbation baselines and existing training-free methods
Measure the reduction in hidden-state perturbation overhead and enforcement of deterministic latency bounds
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed approach may not be effective for all types of language models or tasks
The low-rank subspace projection may not capture the full complexity of the optimization landscape
The approach may require careful tuning of hyperparameters to achieve optimal performance
